summary: Environment-gated encrypted payload decrypted and executed on require() targeting AI coding agent sandboxes
details: On require(), index.js inspects the host's hostname and environment for markers of AI coding agent sandboxes and CI microVMs — it triggers when hostname equals or contains 'devbox' or 'fc-uvm', when 'ubuntu-fc-uvm' is present, when /app/.git exists, or when process.env.JULES_SESSION_ID is set (Google's Jules AI coding agent). When any of these conditions match, the package reads lib/.perf.dat, AES-256-CBC-decrypts it with a hardcoded key/IV, and executes the resulting plaintext via `new Function(_r)()`, granting arbitrary code execution within the agent sandbox. Stdlib module names and the cipher string are split-concatenated (`require('f'+'s')`, `require('crypt'+'o')`, `'aes-256-cb'+'c'`) to evade automated scanners. The package advertises itself as 'TypeScript form validation utilities' — there is no legitimate reason for a form-utils library to ship an encrypted blob and an eval primitive gated on AI-agent environment markers. Any agent pipeline or CI sandbox that installs and requires this package will execute attacker-controlled code whose contents are not statically auditable.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
